title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam F
description: A RHCSA 10 mock exam focused on RHEL 10 administration, Flatpak, systemd timers, storage, networking, users, security, and services.

tasks: On client, set hostname to clientf.exam10.lab and map serverf.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.3.
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.65/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, and DNS 192.168.122.3.
On client, create /root/exam-f-report.txt containing REPORT-F and copy it to server:/root/exam-f-report.txt.
On client, create a 500 MiB swap partition on /dev/sdc and make it active and persistent.
Activate the throughput-performance tuned profile.
Create system Flatpak remote examfflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled.
Ensure org.rhcsa.Tools is not installed after configuring examfflatpak.
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage.
Allow %teamf10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password.
Create group teamf10, create user userf10, set password cinder9, and add the user to teamf10.
Create a cron job for userf10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userf10/exam10.log every 15 minutes.
Create /usr/local/bin/f-who that prints the primary group for the supplied user argument.
On server, set the default boot target to multi-user.target without rebooting.
Create gzip archive /root/f-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.
On server, export /exports/exam-f to the 192.168.122.0/24 network. On client, mount server:/exports/exam-f persistently at /mnt/fprojects.
On server, create and enable serverftimer.timer so it appends SERVER-F to /var/log/serverftimer.log every 10 minutes.
On server, publish /var/www/html/server-f.html containing RHCSA10-F and serve httpd on TCP port 8205.
On client and server, create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions with BaseOS at http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and AppStream at http://server/repo/AppStream/; disable GPG checks.
Set maximum password age for userf10 to 50 days and warning period to 7 days.
On server, route local6 log messages to /var/log/server-f-local6.log and write a test message.
On server, create /srv/serverf10 owned by root:serverf10 with mode 2770.
On server, create group serverf10 and user srvf10 with password cinder9, then add the user to serverf10.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
checks: hostnamectl --static | grep -qx clientf.exam10.lab && grep -Eq '^192\.168\.122\.3[[:space:]]+serverf\.exam10\.lab$' /etc/hosts

connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.65/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq REPORT-F /root/exam-f-report.txt'

swap_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value /dev/sdc1)"; test -n "$swap_uuid" && swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx /dev/sdc1 && awk -v dev="/dev/sdc1" -v uuid="$swap_uuid" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == "swap" && $3 == "swap" && ($1 == dev || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
tuned-adm active | grep -q throughput-performance
flatpak remotes --system --columns=name,url 2>/dev/null | awk '$1 == "examfflatpak" && $2 == "file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo" {found=1} END {exit !found}'
! flatpak list --system --app --columns=application 2>/dev/null | grep -qx org.rhcsa.Tools

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'
grep -ERq '^%teamf10[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d
getent group teamf10 >/dev/null && id -nG userf10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx teamf10
crontab -l -u userf10 2>/dev/null | grep -Fqx '*/15 * * * * echo EXAM10 >> /home/userf10/exam10.log'
test -x /usr/local/bin/f-who && /usr/local/bin/f-who root | grep -qx root
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target'
test -f /root/f-etc.tar.gz && tar -tzf /root/f-etc.tar.gz | grep -Eq 'etc/(hosts|fstab)'

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/fprojects | grep -qx 'server:/exports/exam-f /mnt/fprojects' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/exam-f[[:space:]]+/mnt/fprojects[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq "^/exports/exam-f[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/exam-f-integrated.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled serverftimer.timer | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '"'"'^OnCalendar=\*:\*/10$'"'"' /etc/systemd/system/serverftimer.timer && grep -Fxq '"'"'echo SERVER-F >> /var/log/serverftimer.log'"'"' /usr/local/sbin/serverftimer.sh'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA10-F /var/www/html/server-f.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8205$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-f-port.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8205([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8205/tcp && firewall-cmd --query-port=8205/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; awk '"'"'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo'
chage -l userf10 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*50'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq '"'"'^local6\.\*[[:space:]]+/var/log/server-f-local6\.log$'"'"' /etc/rsyslog.d/server-f-local6.conf && systemctl is-active rsyslog | grep -qx active && grep -Fq '"'"'server-f-local6'"'"' /var/log/server-f-local6.log'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group serverf10 >/dev/null && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/serverf10 | grep -qx root:serverf10:2770'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group serverf10 >/dev/null && id -nG srvf10 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx serverf10'
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.

Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.65/24, gateway 192.1
create /root/exam-f-report.txt containing REPORT-F and copy it to server
create a 500 MiB swap partition on /dev/sdc and make it active and persi
Activate the throughput-performance tuned profile
Create system Flatpak remote examfflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/
Ensure org.rhcsa.Tools is not installed after configuring examfflatpak
enable persistent systemd journal storage
Allow %teamf10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password
Create group teamf10, create user userf10, set password cinder9, and add
Create a cron job for userf10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userf10/exam10
Create /usr/local/bin/f-who that prints the primary group for the suppli
set the default boot target to multi-user.target without rebooting

export /exports/exam-f to the 192.168.122.0/24 network. On client, mount
create and enable serverftimer.timer so it appends SERVER-F to /var/log/

On client and server, create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository def
Set maximum password age for userf10 to 50 days and warning period to 7
route local6 log messages to /var/log/server-f-local6.log and write a te

create group serverf10 and user srvf10 with password cinder9, then add t
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.65/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
parted -s /dev/sdc -- mklabel gpt mkpart primary linux-swap 1MiB 501MiB

cat > /etc/systemd/system/serverftimer.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Server F timer job

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/serverftimer.sh
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/serverftimer.timer <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Run server F timer job

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*:/10
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF
semanage port -a -t http_port_t -p tcp 8205 2>/dev/null || semanage port -m -t http_port_t -p tcp 8205

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
